campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's investment report for March 2026, approved a new policy restricting how city property can be used in relation to federal civil enforcement activities, and discussed efforts to fill open city jobs and improve how the city attracts and keeps employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment transactions for March 2026
New policy prohibiting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to consider objections to cleaning up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the County, and voted to move forward with the abatement work. The council also adopted a new Technology Disruption Policy and approved a process for residents to request reasonable accommodations from the city.
keyTopics: Hazardous property abatement ordered on County-identified properties
New Technology Disruption Policy adopted
Reasonable accommodations request process approved

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and address vegetation management issues. The council declared hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation a public nuisance and approved contracts for landscape and lighting maintenance services.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation declared a public nuisance
Landscape and Lighting District engineer's report approved
Five-year service agreements authorized with Pacific and other vendors

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a brief meeting to finalize the agenda for their May 19 meeting, including deciding on any new items to add and setting specific times for certain discussions. The Council also reviewed the schedule for all City Council meetings in fiscal year 2026-2027 and handled appointments to various city committees and boards.
keyTopics: Finalized May 19 City Council agenda and added any last-minute items
Set the 2026-2027 meeting schedule for the full year
Approved City Council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Reviewed travel request from Council Member Foley

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a procedural notice for Mountain View residents about how to participate in upcoming City Council and board meetings. The city is reminding people how to submit questions in advance and where to find meeting agendas and details.
keyTopics: Submit questions before meetings using the online form
Meeting agendas posted at least 24 hours in advance
Contact City Manager's Office at 650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov for questions
View agendas at City Clerk's Office, 500 Castro Street, Third Floor

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular evening council meeting. The agenda focused on private discussion items during the closed session, with the regular meeting opening with the traditional salute to the flag.
keyTopics: Closed session discussion (specific topics not disclosed publicly)
Small business focus
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial planning session to review Cupertino's proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting also covered rules requiring lobbyists and people trying to influence city decisions to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and discussion
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Public testimony procedures for legislative and administrative matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met on May 14, 2026, primarily to discuss legal procedures and disclosures. The council reviewed important information about time limits for residents or businesses who want to challenge city decisions in court—you have 90 days from the date a decision becomes final to file a lawsuit.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to legally challenge city decisions
Quasi-adjudicative decisions subject to lawsuit procedures
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to approve a five-year plan for city infrastructure spending (2027-2031) and reviewed the operating budget for the next fiscal year. The council also approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan - infrastructure spending priorities for next 5 years
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget - city's day-to-day spending plan
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new parks and trail development
City Council procedures updated - changes to meeting rules and handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story, 72-unit apartment building at 450 Lytton Avenue on a city-owned parking lot. The project would be 100% affordable housing and is seeking special approvals under state law to allow higher density development on the small 0.43-acre parcel.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units on Lytton Avenue site
Replacing 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Six-story building on 0.43-acre parcel
Request for density bonus concessions and waivers
Environmental review under state law
